item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: Given the specificity of the image and no other sources, it is assumed to be user-created and thus compliant.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love AWCY, and I love the concept behind this. This one really left me wanting more - a tale, experiment logs, even a more fleshed-out list of tasks would've been great. Still, big +1 from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: http://www.scp-wiki.net/groups-of-interest#toc1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What does this whole "Are We Cool Yet?" thing mean?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Nice concept but I feel like you could have done so much more with the instruction log. Also, I second the opinion that it could use a subtler quote.

Neutral vote from me.

item.title: Reminds me of something...
item.content:encoded: This definitely reminds me of a movie called Nerve. Anybody know that movie?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Have you played Ingress? It is more immersive and conspiranoid (also more teamwork/strategy based). I stopped playing because I couldn't connect with local players without passing a loyally test (apparently moles are a concern) but if you think playing 1883 would be fun, then witch-hunt paranoia is probably right up your alley!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Still better than Pokemon Go.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My reading of that particular instruction is that to gain the points, you would have to simply not look at the phone after that intruction comes up. Once it switches to the next instruction, it's impossible to not ignore the previous instruction because it's gone. At that moment, you would be awarded 21 points. Of course, if, at any later point in time, you found out how you got those points, you could possibly lose them.

item.title: Staff Post: Page Lock Notice
item.content:encoded:
I went ahead and locked the page because items with lists like these are pretty prone to unauthorized edits.

Readers, editors, passersby, if a page is not tagged with "collaboration", it is not open for new entries from individuals who are not the original author. Anyone who wishes to add new material must obtain permission from the original author or a staff member if said author is unavailable.

item.title: Re: Staff Post: Reversion Notice
item.content:encoded: As has version 17.

item.title: Staff Post: Reversion Notice
item.content:encoded: Version 15 of this article has been reverted, as it made content additions without noted permission.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I would definitely download this! +1! side note: I believe that my Geocaching app has the anomalous compulsion trait in really real life.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: 10/10 would play. I'm sad that the end log seems to have shrunk by the time i got here

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wait… For the "Ignore this task" one, wouldn't you be following the task by ignoring it, henceforth not ignoring it? So how would you gain those precious 21 points? By ignoring it, you would not be ignoring it, since you're actually following and acknowledging what it says. It's like one of those "The next statement is true. The last statement was false." kind of things.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I upvoted because, as said before, AWCY "signs" their works with the phrase "Are we cool yet?", and my personal interpretion of them has them as somewhat more trollish than outright destructive, albeit with some elements of danger always present.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Really liked it overall, but I have some complaints about the list of instructions. Why the reference to Babe Ruth (about whom I know nothing beyond his being a baseball player)? It's not an instruction or a score, so where does it appear on screen? The redactions were disappointing to me: the old 'mundane, bit creepy, really creepy, [EXPUNGED]' cliché at work again. Also the AWCY inclusion is good, but I feel could have been included better (eg: 'Become cool. +-1000).

All of that aside, I'd totally play it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: They aren't terrorists. They're terrartists.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Okay, first off, I like this. +1.

Now for Rants.
The level of GoI hate going on, here and elsewhere, is ludicrous. Yes, they're overdone in new skips. But why are we evaluating entries based on what others are doing? If an SCP uses a GoI in a good way, it's good, no matter how many others have tried. The fact that others use AWCY, often poorly, does not mean that using it is bad. It needs to be an entirely individual judgement, asking whether the subject article fits in with the GoI and if the GoI adds to the depth of the article. here, both are the case.
Furthermore, I hate hate hate when someone says "This doesn't need the [Insert GoI] reference." Of course it doesn't. If it's entirely dependant on that reference, then odds are it's a crappy skip. Exceptions for those foundational entries that make the GoI what it is (Which work because that's the point of them, to establish the tone and feel of the GoI), if it can't stand without the reference it strongly implies that the article is weak overall, and using the GoI to cover that up. Ergo, the statment is absolutely pointless.
And why is it that so often comments boil down to "I liked this until the [GoI] reference." That's not helpful. What was wrong with the GoI? How did it not fit? what about it was inconsistent or detracted from the article? Did it not seem like an item connected to that particular group? … Or do you just hate GoI's entirely? Because that last one is the sense I keep getting. That's like hating 682 because you hate lizards.

Lastly, regarding all this "be cool" argument, i would direct you toa line from the AWCY definition on the GoI page:

the phrase "Are We Cool Yet?" is always present in some way.

This is their signature. Not "Be Cool." If something is very definitely intended to be an AWCY, switching the true phrase to somethign like "Be cool" is undermining the essence of the GoI. If the reference is supposed to be subtle, fine. But in a case like this, where they're signing their work, the phrase NEEDS to be there, or we have to rewrite the GoI page.

*Deep breath*
Okay, i'm done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: And this is why AWCY works, even with the evidence of negative consistences people still try to be cool.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, I noticed that too. Maybe they just wanted to BE COOL. O_O

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Right, I was gonna say… I did that in the parking lot two days ago. No biggie.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, currently it makes it seem like those are the only instructions, and they probably go in that order.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just the still image on the video of this Kickstarter brought this to mind.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm not an anti-AWCY guy, but I'm getting to see why some people are. I was kinda liking it, but then the last log entry just took me straight out of it. -1. (I agree with some others that simply having “Be cool. 200 points” as a goal would've softened the blow a lot.)

This just isn't showy or anarchistic enough to make sense with AWCY. If they're demonstrating how easily people can be manipulated, the goals should be way more conspicuous.

What might be much scarier, and more in-character for AWCY, would be having the goals slowly and (at first) subtly build toward something destructive on a massive scale. AWCY could be using gamification as a way of training an army of subversives. (A crowdsourced Project Mayhem of sorts.)

EDIT: Also, I should think it'd take more than a mild compulsory effect to get people to gouge their own eyes out.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Back in my day all we had was pointy sticks and dead things:
Poke dead thing with pointy stick - 10 Points
Prod pointy stick with dead thing - 5 Points
Eat pointy stick - 18 Points

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Now it says ice cream cone. Are you happy? Cones don't melt! The task is impossible!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
…

This was fantastic, until the last line. I'll keep the upvote, but the AWCY reference just…ugh, this is not a good AWCY reference.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-1883 has come to the attention of the Foundation after the ██/██/████ incident, where at least 72 people in the cities of [REDACTED] irreparably wounded their left eyes with various household objects. Most of them were found to be in possession of a mobile phone with SCP-1883 installed.

…MOST?!

Why did the rest do that?

"Hey, Bob, it's time for the weekly pen-in-the-eye."

"Have fun with that, George! I'm using a kleenex, myself."

"Yer such a pussy, Bob."

"A kleenex that I just wiped my bloody nose with?"

"…still not as cool as a pen."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love it. The AWCY reference doesn't really bother me, the [DATA EXPUNGED] a bit moreso. Doesn't really add much, either that or it goes waaaaay over my head. Still, lovely article. Upvote'd.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love this one, except for the AWCY? reference at the end. The reference felt somewhat forced, since this could easily work without it—but that's just my subjective opinion.

Nice work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Breathe: -200 points

Shit

I really liked this. Until the last paragraph. I really want to upvote this since it is so great, but I really hate AWCY. I always get this image of some band of funnily dressed hipsters that really detriments from the value of the SCP… oh hey, my phone just flicked

Upvote 1883: 100 points

ok

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Actually, something like this could be written for any phone that you can run any type of app on. That's almost any phone you can buy any more. Plus you could do it as a a straight java webpage and have it follow anywhere.

Anywhere.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This wouldn't work on my phone, but I still want it… its like achievements for real life :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I had to downvote.

I didn't want to downvote.

But 'downvote SCP-1883' was 65 points!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I wonder if a -J article about a sentient animate skeleton with a compulsion to "pop out" of random places to scare people would have any potential.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The real skeleton was inside you all along.

But then it popped out.

The trend of skeletal-AWCY tales/SCPs must continue no matter the cost!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Find a horse. Watch horse for 30 minutes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: wait, what about my little pony?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I lost 200 points!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feel like I owe you and zara money

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nthing the "Be cool: 200 points" thing. Preferably with a note that none of the phones they've gotten that instruction on have registered it as having been completed to date.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My personal headcanon says that the instructions literally are "DATA EXPUNGED" - that's not the Foundation's doing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: WWWEEEEEELLLLLL back in myyyy day, us yung'ns had to walk all the way down to the arcade if'n we wanted to play video games! And there weren't no "you win" screen, we just kept blastin' them funny little space whatsits until they was movin' too fast and gotcha. You di'n play to win, you played to show you got further than everyone else! *spittoon* And we liked it just fine.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Then you will be the master

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This has given me the perfect idea for a Sorts-baiting joke SCP

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Rat flavored :B

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What flavor rat?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Imagine eating an ice cream sundae

it is a delicious sundae and you eat it all the way to the bottom and you enjoy it

until you get to the end, when you find a dead rat at the bottom of the cup, ruining your pleasant ice cream experience and your entire day besides

The rat is Are We Cool Yet?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is excellent other than the "are we cool yet?" meme being used.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is so good and I love it so much. Now, what I didn't like:

  1. [DATA EXPUNGED] is bad and thou should not use it.
  2. More generally, I liked the first revision of the instruction log more. I think the log on the main page should be kept short, and all additional instructions should go to a supplement (collaboration?) page.
  3. The "0.5 points" bit bothers me. Score should be kept natural, or the next task will give you i points before you can understand what is happening.
  4. AWCY? reference. I do agree that they would totally do something like that but, ergh, Are We Ironically Overdone Yet? indeed. I do like the more subtle Boa's "Be cool. 200 points" suggestion, however.

Also, zaratustra, you are my favorite SCP writer of this week. Go eat a cake or something.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well played.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Actually that kinda is

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "A Canterlot Wedding" counts as one episode, theres 44 minutes

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really, really like the idea here, but I've got a couple of problems with the execution. Basically, like everyone's been saying, needs a better/longer instruction log. I think it would go over better if the instructions were less… ordered I guess? I know I wouldn't mind the [DATA EXPUNGED] as much if they weren't right next to each other for example.

Also, this is incredibly petty, but either the score in the picture should be lower or it should be made clear that we are only looking at a partial instruction log.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Take every stray cat you see and move it into your home —- +13

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hey, that is a good addition to the list.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this. I think this fits the tone of Are We Cool Yet? well. As with some other comments, I think the redacted entries are superfluous, and fail the Eskobar HTL test: "Throw a turnip at Evander Holyfield from a moving 1988 LeBaron: 400 points"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Consult an alchemist — +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You also have the option of wearing shoes.
